questions.question: Why does good product photography matter for e-commerce?
options: Clear, appealing images influence buying decisions and build trust
questions.reviewHint: Strong product images drive sales, build trust and can reduce returns.

questions.question: A tripod is recommended for product photography because it…
options: Keeps framing consistent and allows sharp images at slower shutter speeds
questions.reviewHint: A tripod gives consistent, repeatable framing and sharpness across a set.

questions.question: A 'sweep' or seamless backdrop is used to…
questions.reviewHint: A seamless backdrop gives a clean, professional, distraction-free background.

questions.question: A diffuser is used in product lighting to…
options: Soften the light and reduce harsh shadows and reflections
questions.reviewHint: Diffusion softens light, reducing hard shadows and hot reflections on products.

questions.question: A reflector is used to…
options: Bounce light back into shadow areas to fill them
Block all light from the subject
questions.reviewHint: Reflectors fill shadows by bouncing light back onto the product.

questions.question: Consistency across a set of product images is important because…
options: It creates a professional, cohesive look across a catalogue
It removes the need for editing
questions.reviewHint: Matching lighting, background and framing gives a cohesive, professional catalogue.

questions.question: Shooting tethered (camera connected to a computer) helps because…
options: You can check focus and detail on a large screen as you shoot
It removes the need for a tripod
questions.reviewHint: Tethering shows images large in real time so you can check sharpness and lighting.

questions.question: For sharp product shots with good depth of field, a common choice is…
options: A moderate to small aperture so the whole product is in focus
questions.reviewHint: Smaller apertures keep the whole product sharp; on a tripod, low ISO keeps quality high.

questions.question: Reflections on shiny products are best controlled by…
options: Diffusing the light and adjusting angles
Using a faster shutter speed only
questions.reviewHint: Soft, diffused light and careful angles tame reflections on glossy surfaces.

questions.question: Why is shooting in RAW useful for product work?
options: It gives more flexibility to correct colour and exposure accurately
It removes the need for lighting
questions.reviewHint: RAW preserves data for accurate colour and exposure correction - important for true product colour.

questions.question: Before shooting, a key preparation step is to…
options: Clean the product and set up the space and lighting
questions.reviewHint: Preparation - cleaning the product, arranging the space and lighting - underpins good results.
